name: Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on your back on a bench.
Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it straight over your chest.
Lower the bar slowly to your mid-chest, keeping elbows at a 45-degree angle.
Push the bar back up powerfully to the starting position, extending your arms fully.

name: Dumbbell Bench Press
instructions: Sit on the edge of a flat bench with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back on the bench, bringing the dumbbells to the sides of your chest with elbows bent.
Press the weights up over your chest until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the weights slowly and under control to the sides of your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Incline Dumbbell Press
instructions: Set an incline bench to approximately 30-45 degrees.
Sit on the bench and lean back, holding dumbbells at the sides of your chest.
Press the dumbbells straight up over your chest, keeping your wrists straight.
Lower the dumbbells slowly until they are near the top of your chest.
Press the weights back up to the starting position.

name: Chest Dips
instructions: Grab the parallel bars and press yourself up to the starting position.
Lean your torso slightly forward and bend your knees.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Push back up through your chest and triceps to return to the starting position.

name: Cable Crossover
instructions: Set pulleys on high position, select resistance, and hold handles in each hand.
Step forward between pulleys, leaning slightly forward, arms extended out to the sides.
Bring your hands together in a wide arc in front of your waist, squeezing your chest.
Return to the starting position slowly, feeling a stretch in your chest.

name: Push-Up
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows.
Push yourself back up to the starting position, keeping your core engaged.

name: Barbell Back Squat
instructions: Rest the barbell on your upper back/traps, gripping it firmly.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointed slightly outward.
Inhale, brace your core, and lower your hips down and back as if sitting in a chair.
Squat down until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to push yourself back up to a standing position.

name: Romanian Deadlift
instructions: Stand holding a barbell or dumbbells in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Keep your feet hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Hinge at your hips, pushing them back as you lower the weight down the front of your legs.
Keep your back flat and chest up, lowering until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings.
Contract your hamstrings and glutes to pull your torso back upright.

name: Leg Press
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine and place your feet flat on the platform, shoulder-width apart.
Lower the safety locks and bend your knees to lower the platform towards your chest.
Stop when your knees reach a 90-degree angle; do not let your lower back lift off the pad.
Push the platform away by extending your legs, but do not lock your knees.

name: Bulgarian Split Squat
instructions: Stand about two feet in front of a bench, holding dumbbells at your sides.
Place the top of your rear foot flat on the bench behind you.
Lower your hips until your rear knee is near the floor and your front thigh is parallel.
Drive through your front heel to return to the standing position.

name: Barbell Deadlift
instructions: Stand with mid-foot under the barbell, feet hip-width apart.
Bend over and grab the bar with a shoulder-width grip.
Bend your knees until your shins touch the bar, keeping your back flat.
Lift your chest, brace your core, and stand up with the weight, keeping the bar close to your body.
Lock out your hips and knees, then lower the bar under control.

name: Barbell Row
instructions: Stand over a barbell with feet hip-width apart. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping back flat.
Grip the bar with an overhand grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Pull the bar up toward your lower chest/belly button, pulling your elbows back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades at the top, then lower the bar slowly to the start.

name: Lat Pulldown
instructions: Sit in the lat pulldown machine and adjust the knee pads.
Grip the bar with a wide overhand grip.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest while leaning slightly back and squeezing your shoulder blades.
Control the bar back up to the starting position, letting your lats stretch.

name: Pull-Up
instructions: Grasp the pull-up bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Hang with arms fully extended. Pull your body up by driving your elbows down.
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar, squeezing your shoulder blades.
Lower your body slowly back to a dead hang position.

name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
instructions: Place one knee and same-side hand on a flat bench for support.
Keep your back flat and parallel to the floor, holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand.
Row the dumbbell up to your hip, keeping your elbow tucked close to your side.
Squeeze your lat at the top, then lower the dumbbell back down fully.

name: Overhead Press
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell at shoulder height with an overhand grip.
Brace your core, squeeze your glutes, and press the bar straight up overhead.
Push your head forward slightly at the top as the bar clears your face.
Lower the bar under control back to your upper chest.

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
instructions: Stand upright holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing inward.
With a slight bend in your elbows, raise your arms out to the sides in a wide arc.
Raise the weights until your arms are parallel to the floor, pinkies pointing slightly up.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
instructions: Sit on an upright bench, holding dumbbells at shoulder height with an overhand grip.
Press the dumbbells straight up over your head until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the weights slowly back down to shoulder level, keeping elbows pointing slightly forward.

name: Barbell Bicep Curl
instructions: Stand straight holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your elbows close to your torso and curl the bar up toward your shoulders.
Squeeze your biceps at the top, then lower the barbell slowly back to the start.

name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing each other (neutral grip).
Keep your elbows tucked and curl the weights up, maintaining the neutral wrist position.
Squeeze your biceps and forearms at the top, then lower slowly.

name: Triceps Pushdown
instructions: Attach a rope or bar to a high cable pulley and grip it with elbows tucked to your sides.
Extend your arms fully, pushing the attachment down toward your thighs.
Contract your triceps hard at the bottom, then return slowly to the starting position.

name: Skull Crushers
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding an EZ-bar or dumbbells over your forehead with arms straight.
Bend at your elbows to lower the weight toward your forehead, keeping upper arms stationary.
Extend your arms to push the weight back up to the starting position.

name: Core
instructions: Kneel on the floor, holding the ab wheel handles under your shoulders.
Roll the wheel forward, extending your body into a straight line without letting your hips sag.
Engage your abs to pull yourself back to the starting position.

name: Hanging Leg Raise
instructions: Hang from a pull-up bar with a straight grip and legs hanging down.
Keeping your legs straight, raise them up until they are parallel to the floor.
Lower your legs slowly under control to prevent swinging.

name: Plank
instructions: Place your forearms on the floor, elbows aligned under shoulders, and extend legs behind.
Support your weight on your toes and forearms, keeping your body in a perfectly straight line.
Engage your abs, glutes, and thighs, holding the position without letting your hips sag.

name: Treadmill Running
instructions: Step onto the treadmill, set your target speed and incline, and start walking.
Transition into a jog or run, maintaining upright posture and swinging your arms naturally.
Cool down by walking for 2-3 minutes before stopping.
